title: CLASS NAMES AND THE INTERNATIONAL NUMBERING SYSTEM FOR FOOD ADDITIVES

sections.section: CLASS NAMES AND THE INTERNATIONAL NUMBERING SYSTEM FOR FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.summary: Section heading: CLASS NAMES AND THE INTERNATIONAL NUMBERING SYSTEM FOR FOOD ADDITIVES (non-substantive heading only).
Document identifier heading: CXG 36-1989 (non-substantive heading only).

sections.section: Adopted in 1989. Revised in 2008. Amended in 2018, 2019, 2021, 2023 and 2024.
sections.summary: Records adoption and amendment history: adopted 1989, revised 2008, and amended in 2018, 2019, 2021, 2023 and 2024.

Explains the International Numbering System (INS) is a harmonized identification system for food additives (alternative to long names), notes that INS inclusion does not imply Codex approval or JECFA evaluation, excludes flavourings, chewing gum bases and dietetic/nutritive additives, and notes enzymes functioning as additives are included in an 1100 series.

sections.section: EXPLANATORY NOTES ON THE LAYOUT OF THE INS
sections.summary: Describes the INS layout: numerical identification (three/four digits with possible alphabetical suffixes), additive names with possible numerical subscripts and optional parenthetical synonyms, a column of grouped technological purposes under consumer-meaningful functional class titles, the manufacturer's responsibility to declare the most descriptive functional class, grouping logic and limits due to list growth, and references where additives with JECFA ADIs and Codex specifications can be found.

sections.section: THE OPEN NATURE OF THE LIST
sections.summary: States the INS is an open, regularly updated identification list subject to additions and removals, and notes related updates for Sections 3 and 4 and that flavourings are covered under separate guidelines (CXG 66-2008).

sections.section: 2. TABLE OF FUNCTIONAL CLASSES, DEFINITIONS AND TECHNOLOGICAL PURPOSES
sections.summary: Table defining functional classes of food additives (e.g., acidity regulator, anticaking agent, antifoaming agent, antioxidant, bleaching agent, emulsifier, preservative, sweetener, etc.) with concise definitions and their technological purposes to serve as a terminology/reference table.

sections.section: 3. INTERNATIONAL NUMBERING SYSTEM FOR FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.summary: Section heading introducing the International Numbering System (INS) for food additives; no substantive text provided.

Numerical catalogue (table) listing INS numbers with corresponding food additive names, functional classes and technological purposes, primarily illustrating colour additives and their classifications.
